title: lavatera rigoi
intro: Lavatera rigoi, also known as the Musk Mallow or Rock Mallow, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its clusters of fragrant, pink to purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of mucilages and flavonoids in its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Lavatera rigoi include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation and pain, and supporting respiratory health due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
